name: White v White
principle: Fairness requires the court to avoid discrimination between breadwinner and homemaker contributions. The case is central to the modern sharing principle.
facts: Long marriage with substantial farming assets. The issue was how to approach division of assets fairly.
examPoint: Use for the proposition that there should be no bias in favour of the money-earner and that equality is a cross-check, not a presumption.

quiz.q: White v White is most associated with which principle?
quiz.explain: White v White is a leading authority on fairness and the sharing principle in financial remedy law.

name: Miller; McFarlane
principle: Identifies needs, compensation and sharing as strands of fairness in financial remedy cases.
facts: Conjoined appeals addressing the principles governing financial provision after divorce.
examPoint: Use when structuring submissions around the three fairness strands.

quiz.q: Miller; McFarlane is commonly cited for which three strands?
quiz.explain: The recognised strands are needs, compensation and sharing.

name: Stack v Dowden
principle: In domestic co-ownership cases, equity usually follows the law unless evidence shows a different common intention.
facts: Unmarried cohabitants disputed beneficial shares in a jointly owned family home.
examPoint: Use for joint-name domestic property disputes and the search for common intention.

quiz.q: Stack v Dowden is most relevant to which issue?
quiz.explain: Stack v Dowden is a leading case on beneficial interests in domestic co-owned property.

name: Jones v Kernott
principle: The court may infer or impute intention when quantifying beneficial interests in a family home.
facts: Former cohabitants disputed their respective beneficial shares after separation.
examPoint: Use with Stack v Dowden when discussing whether shares changed over time.

quiz.q: Jones v Kernott is most associated with what issue?
options: Quantifying beneficial interests in a family home
quiz.explain: Jones v Kernott concerns quantification of beneficial interests after parties' intentions changed.

name: Re H-N and Others
principle: Domestic abuse allegations in child arrangements cases should be considered in their full context, including patterns of coercive and controlling behaviour.
facts: Appeals concerning the approach to fact-finding in private law children proceedings involving domestic abuse allegations.
examPoint: Use when considering whether allegations should be analysed as a pattern of behaviour rather than isolated incidents.

quiz.q: Re H-N is especially relevant to which issue?
options: Domestic abuse allegations in child arrangements proceedings
quiz.explain: Re H-N concerns the approach to domestic abuse allegations and fact-finding in private law children cases.
